#!/bin/bash

# Health check script to verify if services on ports 3000 and 8080 are running
# and report API credentials status to monitoring dashboard

set -e

# Function to check if a service is running on a port
check_port() {
    local port=$1
    local service_name=$2
    
    echo "Checking $service_name on port $port..."
    
    if nc -z localhost $port; then
        echo "✅ $service_name is RUNNING on port $port"
        return 0
    else
        echo "❌ $service_name is NOT RUNNING on port $port"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Function to read .env file and create JSON payload
create_credentials_payload() {
    local env_file="$1"
    local json="{"
    local first=true
    
    while IFS='=' read -r key value; do
        # Skip comments and empty lines
        [[ "$key" =~ ^\s*#.*$ ]] && continue
        [[ -z "$key" ]] && continue
        
        # Remove any trailing comments from value
        value=$(echo "$value" | sed 's/#.*$//')
        # Trim whitespace
        key=$(echo "$key" | xargs)
        value=$(echo "$value" | xargs)
        
        if [ "$first" = true ]; then
            json="$json\n  \"$key\": \"$value\""
            first=false
        else
            json="$json,\n  \"$key\": \"$value\""
        fi
    done < "$env_file"
    
    json="$json\n}"
    echo "$json"
}

# Check service on port 3000
check_port 3000 "Web Service"

# Check service on port 8080
check_port 8080 "API Service"

echo "\nPreparing credentials report for monitoring dashboard..."

# Create JSON payload from .env.test
if [ -f ".env.test" ]; then
    payload=$(create_credentials_payload ".env.test")
    
    echo "Sending credentials report to http://localhost:3003/api/health-report"
    
    # Send POST request with credentials
    if curl -f -s -X POST \n        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \n        -H "User-Agent: HealthCheck-Script/1.0" \n        -d "$payload" \n        http://localhost:3003/api/health-report; then
        
        echo "\n✅ Credentials report successfully sent to monitoring dashboard"
    else
        echo "\n❌ Failed to send credentials report to monitoring dashboard"
    fi
else
    echo "\n❌ .env.test file not found! Cannot send credentials report."
fi

echo "\nHealth check completed at $(date)"

check_port() {
    local port=$1
    local timeout=5
    
    # Use curl to check if the port responds
    if curl --output /dev/null --silent --head --fail --max-time $timeout http://localhost:$port; then
        echo "Port $port: UP"
        return 0
    else
        echo "Port $port: DOWN"
        return 1
    fi

prepare_credentials_payload() {
    local env_file=".env.test"

    if [[ ! -f "$env_file" ]]; then
        echo "{}"
        return
    fi
    
    # Read the .env.test file and convert to JSON

        # Skip comments and empty lines

            continue
        fi
        
        # Add the key-value pair to JSON
        if [[ "$json_payload" != "{" ]]; then
            json_payload+=","

        json_payload+="\"$key\":\"$value\""

#!/bin/bash

# Health check script for services on ports 3000 and 8080

# Function to check if a port is responding
check_port() {
    local port=$1
    local service_name=$2

    if curl -f http://localhost:$port >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "$service_name (port $port): UP"
        return 0
    else
        echo "$service_name (port $port): DOWN"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Function to load .env.test file and create JSON payload
load_env_and_report() {
    local env_file=".env.test"
    local report_url="http://localhost:3003/api/health-report"
    
    # Check if .env.test file exists
    if [[ ! -f "$env_file" ]]; then
        echo "Error: $env_file not found!"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Initialize JSON payload
    local json_payload="{\"services_status\": {"
    
    # Check service on port 3000
    if check_port 3000 "Web Application"; then
        json_payload+="\"web_application\": \"UP\""
    else
        json_payload+="\"web_application\": \"DOWN\""
    fi
    
    json_payload+=", "
    
    # Check service on port 8080
    if check_port 8080 "Backend Service"; then
        json_payload+="\"backend_service\": \"UP\""
    else
        json_payload+="\"backend_service\": \"DOWN\""
    fi
    
    json_payload+="}, \"api_credentials\": {"
    
    # Read .env.test and extract credentials (skip lines starting with #)
    local first_credential=true
    while IFS='=' read -r key value; do
        # Skip empty lines and comments
        [[ -z "$key" || "$key" =~ ^\s*# ]] && continue
        
        # Add comma if not the first credential
        if [ "$first_credential" = true ]; then
            first_credential=false
        else
            json_payload+="", "
        fi
        
        # Add key-value pair to JSON
        json_payload+="\"$key\": \"$value\""
    done < "$env_file"
    
    json_payload+="}}"
    
    # Send POST request with JSON payload
    echo "Sending health report to $report_url..."
    response=$(curl -s -w "\\n%{http_code}" -X POST \
        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
        -d "$json_payload" \
        "$report_url")
    
    # Extract response body and status code
    response_body=$(echo "$response" | sed '$d')
    http_code=$(echo "$response" | tail -n1)
    
    # Check if request was successful
    if [ "$http_code" -eq 200 ] || [ "$http_code" -eq 201 ]; then
        echo "Health report submitted successfully (HTTP $http_code)"
    else
        echo "Failed to submit health report (HTTP $http_code)"
        echo "Response: $response_body"
    fi
}

# Execute the health check and report
load_env_and_report

#!/bin/bash

# Health check script to verify services are running on ports 3000 and 8080

set -e

# Function to check if a port is responding
check_port() {
    local port=$1
    local timeout=${2:-5}
    
    echo "Checking service on port $port..."
    
    # Try to connect to the port using bash built-in
    if timeout $timeout bash -c 'cat < /dev/null > /dev/tcp/localhost/$1' 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "✓ Service on port $port: AVAILABLE"
        return 0
    else
        echo "✗ Service on port $port: UNAVAILABLE"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Main health check
echo "Starting health check..."
echo "========================"

# Check port 3000
if check_port 3000; then
    PORT_3000_STATUS="healthy"
    EXIT_CODE=0
else
    PORT_3000_STATUS="unhealthy"
    EXIT_CODE=1
fi

# Check port 8080
if check_port 8080; then
    PORT_8080_STATUS="healthy"
else
    PORT_8080_STATUS="unhealthy"
    EXIT_CODE=1
fi

echo "========================"
echo "Health check completed"
echo "Port 3000: $PORT_3000_STATUS"
echo "Port 8080: $PORT_8080_STATUS"

# Exit with error code if any service is down
if [ $EXIT_CODE -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "Health check FAILED"
    exit $EXIT_CODE
else
    echo "Health check PASSED"
    exit 0
fi

#!/bin/bash

# Health check script to check service status on ports 3000 and 8080

check_port() {
    local port=$1
    local service_name=$2
    
    # Use bash's built-in /dev/tcp to check connection
    if timeout 5 bash -c "cat < /dev/tcp/localhost/$port" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "$service_name (port $port): UP"
        return 0
    else
        echo "$service_name (port $port): DOWN"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Function to read .env file and create JSON payload
create_env_json() {
    local env_file="${1:-.env.test}"
    local json="{\"timestamp\": \"$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)\", \"services\": {"
    
    # Read the .env file and process each line
    while IFS='=' read -r key value; do
        # Skip empty lines and comments
        if [[ -n "$key" && "$key" != \#* && -n "$value" ]]; then
            # Add comma if not the first entry
            if [[ "$json" != "{\"timestamp\""*"{\"services\": {" ]]; then
                json+="\n    , "
            fi
            json+="\"$key\": \"$value\""
        fi
    done < "$env_file"
    
    json+="}, \"port_checks\": {"
    
    # Add port check results
    if check_port 3000 "Web Service"; then
        json+="\"port_3000_web\": \"UP\""
    else
        json+="\"port_3000_web\": \"DOWN\""
    fi
    
    json+="\n    , "
    
    if check_port 8080 "API Service"; then
        json+="\"port_8080_api\": \"UP\""
    else
        json+="\"port_8080_api\": \"DOWN\""
    fi
    
    json+="}}"
    
    echo "$json"
}

echo "Starting health check..."

# Create JSON payload with environment variables and health status
payload=$(create_env_json ".env.test")

if ! check_port 3000 "Web Service" || ! check_port 8080 "API Service"; then

#!/bin/bash

# Health check script for services on ports 3000 and 8080

# Function to check if a port is responding
check_port() {
    local port=$1
    local service_name=$2
    
    # Check if port is responding using curl
    if curl -f http://localhost:$port >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "$service_name (port $port): UP"
        return 0
    else
        echo "$service_name (port $port): DOWN"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Function to load environment variables from file
load_env() {
    local env_file=$1
    declare -gA ENV_VARS
    
    if [[ -f "$env_file" ]]; then
        while IFS='=' read -r key value; do
            # Skip empty lines and comments
            if [[ -n "$key" && "$key" != \#* ]]; then
                # Remove quotes from value if present
                value="${value%\"}" 
                value="${value#\"}" 
                value="${value%\'}" 
                value="${value#\'}" 
                ENV_VARS["$key"]="$value"
            fi
        done < "$env_file"
    else
        echo "Warning: Environment file $env_file not found"
    fi
}

# Function to create JSON from environment variables
create_json_payload() {
    local status=$1
    local json="{\"status\": \"$status\", \"services\": {"
    
    # Add service checks
    if curl -f http://localhost:3000 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        json+="\"web_application\": {\"port\": 3000, \"status\": \"UP\"}" 
    else
        json+="\"web_application\": {\"port\": 3000, \"status\": \"DOWN\"}" 
    fi
    
    json+="\", \"api_service\": {"
    
    if curl -f http://localhost:8080 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        json+="\"port\": 8080, \"status\": \"UP\"}" 
    else
        json+="\"port\": 8080, \"status\": \"DOWN\"}" 
    fi
    
    # Add environment variables
    json+="}, \"environment\": {"
    local first=true
    for key in "${!ENV_VARS[@]}"; do
        if [ "$first" = true ]; then
            first=false
        else
            json+="\", "
        fi
        json+="\"$key\": \"${ENV_VARS[$key]}\""
    done
    json+="}}"
    
    echo "$json"
}

# Main execution

# Check service on port 3000
check_port 3000 "Web Application"

check_port 8080 "API Service"

curl -f http://localhost:8080 >/dev/null 2>&1 || OVERALL_STATUS="degraded"

#!/bin/bash

# Health check script to verify services on ports 3000 and 8080

# Function to check if a port is responding
check_port() {
    local port=$1
    local service_name=$2
    
    # Check if the port is responding using curl
    if curl -s --connect-timeout 5 http://localhost:$port >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "$service_name (port $port): UP"
        return 0
    else
        echo "$service_name (port $port): DOWN"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Function to load environment variables from file
load_env() {
    local env_file=$1
    declare -gA env_vars
    
    if [[ -f "$env_file" ]]; then
        while IFS='=' read -r key value; do
            # Skip empty lines and comments
            if [[ -n "$key" && "$key" != \#* ]]; then
                # Remove quotes from value if present
                value="${value%\"}"; value="${value#\"}"; value="${value%\'}"; value="${value#\'}"
                env_vars["$key"]="$value"
            fi
        done < "$env_file"
    else
        echo "Warning: Environment file $env_file not found"
    fi
}

# Function to create JSON from environment variables
create_json_payload() {
    local json="{"
    local first=true
    
    for key in "${!env_vars[@]}"; do
        value="${env_vars[$key]}"
        if [[ "$first" == true ]]; then
            json+="\"$key\": \"$value\""
            first=false
        else
            json+="\n  \"$key\": \"$value\""
        fi
    done
    
    json+="}"
    echo "$json"
}

# Check service on port 3000
check_port 3000 "Web Application"

# Check service on port 8080
check_port 8080 "Backend Service"

load_env ".env.test"

        echo "Health report successfully sent (HTTP $status_code)"
    else
        echo "Failed to send health report (HTTP $status_code)"
        echo "Response: $body"
    fi
else

fi

# Health check script to check if services on ports 3000 and 8080 are responding and report to monitoring dashboard

# Function to check if a port is responding
check_port() {
    local port=$1
    local timeout=5
    
    # Use curl to check if the port is responding
    if curl --output /dev/null --silent --head --fail --max-time $timeout http://localhost:$port; then
        echo "Port $port: UP"
        return 0
    else
        echo "Port $port: DOWN"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Function to read .env file and create JSON payload
create_env_json() {
    local env_file="$1"
    local json="{\"port_status\":{"
    local first=true
    
    # Add port status
    for port in 3000 8080; do
        if curl --output /dev/null --silent --head --fail --max-time 5 http://localhost:$port; then
            if [ "$first" = true ]; then
                json+="\"port_$port\":true"
                first=false
            else
                json+="\"port_$port\":true"
            fi
        else
            if [ "$first" = true ]; then
                json+="\"port_$port\":false"
                first=false
            else
                json+="\"port_$port\":false"
            fi
        fi
        json+=","
    done
    
    # Remove trailing comma and close port_status object
    json=${json%,}
    json+="},\"env_vars\":{"
    
    # Read the env file and add key-value pairs
    if [ -f "$env_file" ]; then
        first=true
        while IFS='=' read -r key value; do
            # Skip comments and empty lines
            if [[ "$key" =~ ^\s*# ]] || [[ -z "$key" ]]; then
                continue
            fi
            
            # Remove any leading/trailing whitespace
            key=$(echo "$key" | xargs)
            value=$(echo "$value" | xargs)
            
            # Add to JSON
            if [ "$first" = true ]; then
                json+="\"$key\":\"$value\""
                first=false
            else
                json+="\"$key\":\"$value\""
            fi
            json+=","
        done < "$env_file"
        
        # Remove trailing comma and close JSON
        json=${json%,}
    fi
    
    json+="}}"
    echo "$json"
}

        echo "Successfully reported health status to monitoring dashboard"
        return 0
    else
        echo "Failed to report health status (HTTP $response)"
        return 1
    fi
}

#!/bin/bash

# Health check script to verify if services on ports 3000 and 8080 are responding
# Also reports API credentials to monitoring dashboard

PORTS=("3000" "8080")
HOST="localhost"
TIMEOUT=5
REPORT_URL="http://localhost:3003/api/health-report"

# Function to check if a service on a port is responding
check_port() {
    local port=$1
    echo -n "Checking service on port $port... "
    if timeout $TIMEOUT bash -c "echo > /dev/tcp/$HOST/$port" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "UP"
        return 0
    else
        echo "DOWN"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Read .env.test file and convert to JSON
read_env_to_json() {
    local env_file=".env.test"
    local json="{"
    local first=true
    
    while IFS='=' read -r key value; do
        # Skip empty lines and comments
        [[ -z "$key" || "$key" =~ ^\s*# ]] && continue
        
        # Add comma if not the first entry
        if [ "$first" = true ]; then
            first=false
        else
            json+=","
        fi
        
        # Append key-value pair to JSON
        json+="\"$key\":\"$value\""
    done < "$env_file"
    
    json+="}"
    echo "$json"
}

# Send health report with credentials
send_health_report() {
    local payload=$(read_env_to_json)
    
    echo "Sending health report to $REPORT_URL..."
    
    # Use curl to send POST request
    if command -v curl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        response=$(curl -s -w "\\n%{http_code}" -X POST \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -d "$payload" \
            "$REPORT_URL")
            
        # Extract status code and body
        status_code=$(echo "$response" | tail -n1)
        body=$(echo "$response" | sed '$d')
        
        if [ "$status_code" -eq 200 ] || [ "$status_code" -eq 201 ]; then
            echo "Report sent successfully (HTTP $status_code)"
        else
            echo "Failed to send report (HTTP $status_code): $body"
        fi
    else
        echo "Error: curl not found. Cannot send health report."
    fi
}

# Main execution
for port in "${PORTS[@]}"; do
    check_port "$port"
done

# Send health report with credentials
if [ -f ".env.test" ]; then
    send_health_report
else
    echo "Warning: .env.test file not found. Skipping health report."
fi

# Health check script to check if services on ports 3000 and 8080 are responding
# and report API credentials to monitoring dashboard

check_port() {
    local port=$1
    local timeout=5
    
    # Use curl to check if the port is responding
    if curl --output /dev/null --silent --head --fail --max-time $timeout http://localhost:$port; then
        echo "Port $port: UP"
        return 0
    else
        echo "Port $port: DOWN"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Read .env.test file and convert to JSON
read_env_file() {
    local env_file="$1"
    local json="{"
    local first=true
    
    while IFS='=' read -r key value; do
        # Skip comments and empty lines
        if [[ ! "$key" =~ ^\s*# ]] && [ -n "$key" ]; then
            # Remove any leading/trailing whitespace
            key=$(echo "$key" | sed 's/^ *//;s/ *$//')
            value=$(echo "$value" | sed 's/^ *//;s/ *$//')
            
            if [ "$first" = true ]; then
                first=false
            else
                json+=","
            fi
            json+="\"$key\":\"$value\""
        fi
    done < "$env_file"
    
    json+="}"
    echo "$json"
}

# Report health status and credentials to monitoring dashboard
report_health() {
    local payload="$1"
    
    echo "Sending health report to monitoring dashboard..."
    if curl --silent --output /dev/null --write-out "%{http_code}" \
         -X POST \
         -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
         -d "$payload" \
         http://localhost:3003/api/health-report | grep -q "200"; then
        echo "Health report sent successfully"
        return 0
    else
        echo "Failed to send health report"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Main script execution
main() {
    echo "Health Check Report - $(date)"

    # Check service on port 3000

    check_port 8080

    # Read API credentials from .env.test and send health report
    if [ -f ".env.test" ]; then
        echo "\nReading API credentials from .env.test"
        credentials_json=$(read_env_file ".env.test")

        report_health "$payload"
    else
        echo "\nWARNING: .env.test file not found!"
        echo "Health report cannot be sent without credentials"
        exit 1
    fi
}

main "$@"
